name: Karen Gillan
description:
Karen Gillan, born November 28 1987, in Inverness, Scotland,  is a versatile actress known for her work in both film and television. Raised in Inverness and later relocating to Edinburgh, her passion for acting started early. Beginning with local theater productions, she got her first TV role in the Scottish drama series "Rebus" in 2006. Gillan gained widespread recognition for her portrayal of Amy Pond in the long-running series "Doctor Who" from 2010 onwards. Transitioning to the big screen, she played Nebula in Marvel's "Guardians of the Galaxy" series, showcasing her acting range. Beyond Marvel, she appeared in films like "Not Another Happy Ending" (2013) and the "Jumanji" series. Gillan's talent earned her nominations and awards, including a Saturn Award nomination for "Doctor Who" and a National Television Award for Outstanding Drama Performance in 2012. In addition to acting, Gillan ventured into writing and directing, creating the well-received film "The Party's Just Beginning" in 2018. Throughout her career, she has proven herself as a versatile and talented actress, taking on diverse roles. In 2023, she starred in "Late Bloomers," praised for her performance by Damon Wise of Deadline Hollywood. Gillan also appeared in "Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3," began filming "Sleeping Dogs" in Australia, and has an upcoming role in the ITVX comedy drama "Douglas Is Cancelled." With her dedication to storytelling, Gillan continues to captivate audiences globally.
